updates.tree_path: Root > MealPlan_Version 1 > Father > Day 7 > Dinner > DIY Taco Night — Maple-Kissed Skillet

short_description.from: A quick, customizable taco night featuring lean beef, corn tortillas, fresh veggies, cheddar, and salsa.
short_description.to: Rich, slow-braised beef tacos served with a savory consommé for dipping — tender shredded beef tucked in warm corn tortillas and topped with onion and cilantro for a deeply flavorful taco night.

nutrition_note.from: Lean beef provides high‑quality protein; corn tortillas supply carbohydrates; lettuce and tomato add volume and micronutrients; cheddar contributes calcium and fat; salsa offers flavor with relatively low calories. To boost fiber, use whole‑grain tortillas or add beans; to reduce sodium, opt for low‑sodium salsa and lighter cheese.
nutrition_note.to: Birria tacos are protein-rich from the braised beef. Using fattier cuts yields more flavor and a richer consommé but increases saturated fat; trim visible fat or skim fat from the cooking liquid to reduce fat content. Serving on corn tortillas keeps portioned carbohydrates; add cabbage or radish to boost fiber and volume. Watch the consommé and braising liquid for sodium — use low-sodium broth or reduce added salt if needed.

meal_suggestion.from: Assemble made‑to‑order tacos: warm corn tortillas, top with lean beef, shredded lettuce, diced tomato, cheddar, and salsa. Optional lime, avocado, or light sour cream. Pair with a simple side salad or beans for extra fiber.
meal_suggestion.to: Prepare a birria-style beef by searing beef chuck and braising it slowly with rehydrated dried chiles (such as guajillo and ancho), onion, garlic, cumin, oregano, and beef broth until very tender. Remove meat, shred, and reduce the braising liquid to make a flavorful consommé. Lightly dip corn tortillas in rendered cooking juices or a bit of oil in a hot skillet, warm, then fill with shredded beef. Top tacos with finely chopped white onion and cilantro and a squeeze of lime. Serve with a small bowl of warm consommé for dipping and optional crumbled queso fresco or sliced radish and shredded cabbage on the side.

updates.reasoning: Transformed the Father's maple-kissed taco skillet into Birria tacos. Rewrote name, description, meal suggestion, ingredients, and nutrition note to reflect the new dish and cooking method.
